_about: Adversarial-review sign-off registry. Coverage EXPANSION is gated the same way REMOVAL is: every CHECK id in coverage_lock.json must be covered by a sign-off here recording that an INDEPENDENT reviewer re-read the pinned spec at the check's cited clause and confirmed the check binds the right subject, cites faithfully, and is not over-strict. verify_review_signoffs.py fails the build if a locked CHECK id has no valid sign-off — so a future session cannot grow coverage while skipping the review that keeps it honest. A sign-off needs: reviewer, date, spec_reverified=true, notes (what was checked), and the ids it covers. Add new checks to a NEW sign-off batch after their review; never backfill an unreviewed id into an existing batch.

signoffs.reviewer: independent adversarial review, waves 1-3 (skeptic agent re-reading each pinned clause)
signoffs.notes: The initial coverage across all three pinned versions was built via parallel TDD waves and then adversarially re-reviewed in three passes for wrong subject-binding, citation stretch, over-strictness, vacuous kill-proofs, and version leakage. Findings F1..Fn were applied (including removing the unsound SIG-035). Every id below was live and kill-rate-validated at the time of the baseline lock. Subsequent additions must be signed off in their own batch.

signoffs.reviewer: independent adversarial review (agent re-read pinned checkout-mcp.md + catalog/mcp.md against each check)
signoffs.notes: Added 3 MCP JSON-RPC-2.0 transport-conformance checks for 2026-04-08 (mcp.checkout_via_tools_call, mcp.error_conformance, mcp.catalog_via_tools_call) covering MCP-003/004/006. Independent review verified the structuredContent path (checkout-mcp.md:212-213 / catalog-mcp.md:160-164) and jsonrpc:'2.0' requirement, and REFUTED two over-strictness bugs which were then FIXED before shipping: (1) checkout check no longer requires top-level id/status (checkout-response shape, legitimately absent from an all-out-of-stock error result) — asserts only the JSON-RPC envelope + ucp; (2) error check no longer requires HTTP 200 (spec pins the JSON-RPC body, not the transport status) and tests an unknown METHOD (unambiguous JSON-RPC-2.0 -32601) rather than an unknown tool name (which checkout-mcp.md leaves to MCP convention). All 3 clean-pass + kill-safe on the controlled fixture.

signoffs.reviewer: independent adversarial review (agent re-read catalog/mcp.md, order-mcp.md, checkout-a2a.md per check)
signoffs.notes: Added mcp.get_product_tool (MCP-001), mcp.get_order_tool (MCP-005), a2a.checkout_in_datapart (A2A-001) for 2026-04-08 — closing the server-testable MCP/A2A frontier (only MCP-002 remains, client-side/manual). Fixture gained get_product routing + an A2A message/send endpoint returning the checkout in a DataPart keyed a2a.ucp.checkout; ctx gained has_a2a/a2a_endpoint; engine gained a2a_call. Independent review verified citations exact (catalog/mcp.md:693 both-tools, order-mcp.md:292 get_order, checkout-a2a.md:144 a2a.ucp.checkout) and subjects server-side. It REFUTED an A2A over-strictness bug (predicate accepted only the Message shape, would false-fail the spec-permitted Task shape at checkout-a2a.md:115-116) — FIXED before shipping: p_a2a_checkout_datapart now recursively finds the DataPart across Message result.parts, Task status.message.parts, and artifacts[].parts (unit-tested: CLEAN on all 3 shapes, DEVIATION on missing-datapart / ucp-less checkout). MCP-001's both-tools split (get_product here + lookup_catalog via mcp.catalog_lookup) and the A2A structured-request assumption are documented in-code. All 3 clean-pass + kill-safe on the controlled fixture.

Re-read ap2-mandates.md at each cited clause. PAY-034 (L106, include merchant_authorization in all checkout responses) + PAY-039 (L140, embed under ap2.merchant_authorization as JWS detached content) -> payment.ap2_authorization_present: binds the merchant_authorization field, asserts detached-JWS shape (header..sig); mutations drop it / set non-JWS / empty ap2 are all caught (3/3 kill-safe). PAY-031 (L29, signature proves terms authentic) + PAY-040 (L172, covers header+payload, alg-substitution defense) + PAY-043 (L218, JCS RFC 8785) -> payment.ap2_authorization_authentic: verifies the detached JWS over JCS(checkout minus ap2) with an ES256-required header. Not over-strict: accepts a key-reordered but JCS-equal payload (proven at the ap2-crypto gate). Coverage split stated honestly: the check's own mutations prove PAYLOAD coverage (currency/quantity/amount edits rejected); HEADER/alg-substitution coverage (PAY-040) is enforced by the predicate and proven by the ap2-crypto gate's alg-swap vector, since the engine cannot re-sign. Fixture signature is genuine (generated by common/crypto, re-verified each run by the checks + ap2-crypto gate). Enforce-side AP2 MUSTs (PAY-035/038/045/047) deliberately left as gaps — no AP2 reference server exists (ops/scope-ap2-mandate-cluster). VALIDATED vs INDEPENDENT oracles (2026-07-16): JCS passes all 6 official RFC 8785 vectors (fixed a number-serialization + UTF-16 key-order bug found by them); JWS-detached interops both ways with the `cryptography` lib. Spec allows alg ES256/ES384/ES512 — ES384/ES512 route to INCONCLUSIVE (not a false deviation), invalid algs to DEVIATION.
